summary: Current optimize tests prove a bounded optimize route that starts from explicit packets, carries held-out results into search input, runs candidate mutation through an eval-test backed held-out/full-gate route, records before/after decision artifacts, and emits reopenable optimize proposal and revision packets.

evidenceScope.targetSurface: Cautilus optimize packet flow and held-out eval-backed search route
evidenceScope.claimBoundary: This bundle supports only the current deterministic optimize flow: packet assembly, held-out search input, eval-test backed candidate evaluation, selected candidate improvement over seed in a temp repo, and reopenable proposal/revision artifacts.
notClaimed: External model quality improves in arbitrary consumer repos
Every optimize target type is covered beyond the checked prompt-target route
A human has accepted the optimized prompt for production
Repeated live GEPA-style search with provider APIs is covered
The optimizer may skip held-out, comparison, or full-gate proof to make an improvement look successful
Fixture-backed temporary repo proof is equivalent to every product-owned app runner's real production behavior

protectedBehaviors: optimize prepare-input, propose, and build-artifact produce durable optimize input, proposal, and revision packets
optimize search prepare-input preserves the held-out scenario set from explicit held-out results
optimize search run invokes eval-test for held-out and full-gate candidate checks in a temporary repo
an eval-backed mutation candidate can beat the seed candidate for the selected behavior
the selected mutation candidate records evaluation artifacts, a held-out evaluation matrix score, and a passing full-gate checkpoint
optimize propose --from-search preserves the search result file and target file references
optimize input packets are assembled from explicit report, review summary, scenario history, target, and budget evidence
optimize proposals stay bounded to one revision plan and retain held-out, comparison, and structured review stop conditions
revision artifacts can be reopened from proposal and input packets
checked-in optimize and optimize-search example packets match their schemas

decision.reason: The executed CLI and packet-flow tests prove the current optimize loop can reopen explicit packets, carry held-out evidence into search, evaluate candidate mutations through eval-test, select an improved candidate in a controlled route, and retain proposal/revision proof artifacts.
